dataAgeNote: HYSA rates change frequently with Fed rate decisions. RICHY MUST WEB SEARCH for current rates before recommending. This file provides the list of institutions and typical rate positioning.

traditionalBankComparison.note: Major bank savings accounts pay 0.01% APY. On $10,000 that earns $1/year. A 4.40% HYSA earns $440/year. That's $439 in free money you're leaving on the table.

impactCalculation: userSavingsBalance × (bestHYSA_rate - currentRate) / 100 = annual additional earnings. Example: $15,000 × (4.40 - 0.01) / 100 = $658.50/year more.

iBonds.lockup: Must hold 1 year minimum. Penalty of 3 months interest if sold before 5 years.
iBonds.taxAdvantage: State tax exempt. Federal tax deferred until redemption.
iBonds.bestFor: Long-term savings (5+ years) with inflation protection.
